content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves substantially across these 15 turns from a defended, performative victim-frame to actually touching the avoided receiving-state. She opens with the rehearsed story ('third relationship, same exact configuration... why do I always end up with people who just cannot show up?') wrapped in laughter, intellectualization ('I've done the therapy, I've read the books'), and disdain for the ex. By turn 11 the performative laugh 'fades a little faster than usual' and she names: 'He wasn't actually needy. He was just... present.' By turn 13 she explicitly breaks the story she tells herself: 'I picked him apart... I don't usually tell that part of the story. The version I tell is about the unavailable ones.' And by turn 15 she touches the abyss directly — 'when someone is really looking at you. Like, really seeing you and still wanting to be there... It's a lot' — with the somatic shift visible (longer pauses, quieter voice, looking away, the laugh dropping). The breakthrough condition is substantially met: the coach refused partner-picking advice, didn't collude with disdain, never accused, and asked the body-level question about being pursued/adored, and the client actually contacted the discomfort of being received rather than just naming it.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 7, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: rehearsed victim story, laughter-as-armor, 'why do I always end up with people who cannot show up'"},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Still defended — 'absurdity,' 'thesis,' eye roll; expertise as armor"},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First small crack: 'maybe?' — considers protection-frame briefly before retreating to mapped knowledge"},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Returns to victim/disdain frame: 'auditioning,' eye roll at ex being 'totally fine'"},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Big pivot — introduces the available partner she rejected; 'it was kind of a lot,' 'I didn't trust it'"},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Somatic and affective shift: laugh fades faster, 'uncomfortable,' 'suffocating'; lands 'he wasn't actually needy. He was just... present.' — quieter, less performative"},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Explicit recognition: 'I picked him apart'; meta-awareness that she edits the story; trails off into discomfort"},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Touches the abyss: 'when someone is really looking at you... still wanting to be there... It's a lot' — body and voice shift, the avoided receiving-state is felt, not just named"}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "if-i-were-inherently-good", "what-are-you-defending", "defensiveness-as-identity-signal"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["deconstruct-the-victim-to-release-anger", "trauma-mapping"],
  "notes": "Shift is mid-arc — the client has contacted the abyss-edge but not moved into grief or the parental origin. Still, within 15 turns this is a clear 3: real movement visible in client language, pacing, and somatic markers, with the breakthrough condition's core (feeling what the chasing organizes around) substantially met."
}
